title: Chest X-ray result review

investigations.text: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.
investigations.rationale: Supports qualified follow-up chest radiography after pneumonia for chest radiograph result review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected chest-radiograph review in cough assessment for chest radiograph result review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Study date and indication, technique, findings, comparison, symptoms, diagnosis, incidental findings, urgency, referral and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chest radiograph result review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
